sections.paragraph:
ἀμέθυστος, ου, ἡ (verbal adj. derived fr. μεθύω; Pliny, HN 37, 121; Plut., Mor. 15b) or ὁ (PHolm δ 3) amethyst (Ex 28:19; Ezk 28:13; Jos., Bell. 5, 234 [ἀμέθυστος; ἀμέθυσος v.l.], Ant. 3, 168 [ἀμέθυσος; so also Psellus, in Les Lapidaires p. 201, 18]; AClausing, Glotta 20, ’32, 292: has the color of wine that is mixed w. water and does not make one drunk) Rv 21:20 (vv.ll. ἀμέθυσος, ἀμεθύστινος).—MBauer, Edelsteinkunde (3d ed. by KSchlossmacher) ’32; EJourdain, The Twelve Stones in the Apc.: ET 22, 1911, 448–50; JMyres, Precious Stones: EncBibl IV 4799–812; CCooper, The Precious Stones of the Bible 1925.—DELG s.v. μέθυ.

adj. adj. = adjective

  1. fr. = from

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

PHolm PHolm = Papyrus Graecus Holmiensis—List 4

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

Psellus Psellus , History, XI a.d.—List 5

Les Lapidaires Les Lapidaires , s. Cyranides—List 5

vv.ll. vv.ll. = variae lectiones (variant readings)

  1. ed. = edited (by), edition

Apc Apc = Apocalypse, but Rv is used for the last book of NT

ET ET = Expository Times—List 6

EncBibl EncBibl = Encyclopaedia Biblica—List 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 52
